zh.Steford, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondham於1445年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾維蒙達哈姆時，她的父親Thomas Berkeley32歲，母親Petronella Brokesby30歲。她於1460年與Sir William Hughes Sr在英國林可爾史爾斯利阿福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1504年12月21日在英國林可爾史爾謝普林加姆 煙雨 波頓 安德 比爾索普享年59歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾謝普林加姆 煙雨 波頓 安德 比爾索普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondham于1445年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾維蒙達哈姆时，她的父亲Thomas Berkeley32岁，母亲Petronella Brokesby30岁。她于1460年与Sir William Hughes Sr在英国林可爾史爾斯利阿福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1504年12月21日在英国林可爾史爾謝普林加姆 煙雨 波頓 安德 比爾索普享年59岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾謝普林加姆 煙雨 波頓 安德 比爾索普。

nameMeanings.name: Berkeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places so named, such as Berkeley in Gloucestershire, named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scottish Barclay .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of   Berkowitz  .

variants.name: Barclay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name of English origin, from any of several places so called, such as Berkley in Somerset or Berkeley in Gloucestershire, both named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The surname was brought to Scotland from southwestern England in the 12th century; Walter de Berchelai or Berkelai was Chamberlain of Scotland in 1165. It was long assumed that they were from the family that held Berkeley Castle in Gloucestershire, but the historian G. W. S. Barrow (1973, The Kingdom of the Scots) adduced evidence that they are more probably from Berkley in Somerset.

variants.name: Berkley
meanings: English: variant of   Berkeley  . Compare Scottish   Barclay  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondham was born in 1445, in Wymondham, Leicestershire, England, her father, Thomas Berkeley, was 32 and her mother, Petronella Brokesby, was 30. She married Sir William Hughes Sr in 1460, in Sleaford, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 21 December 1504, in Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondham est née en 1445, à Wymondham, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Berkeley, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Petronella Brokesby, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Hughes Sr en 1460, à Sleaford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 décembre 1504, à Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1460 in Sleaford, Lincolnshire, England Sir William Hughes Sr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 21. Lei sposato Sir William Hughes Sr nel 1460, nel Sleaford, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondhamが1445年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアウイモンドハムで生まれた時，父Thomas Berkeleyは32歳，母Petronella Brokesbyは30歳であった。1460年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスレアフォードでSir William Hughes Srと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1504年12月21日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアセンプリンガム ウィス ポイントン アンド バーソルペで59歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアセンプリンガム ウィス ポイントン アンド バーソルペにて埋葬された。

ko.Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe,: 영국 링코실르 젬프링함 위스 포인턴 앤드 비르소
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondham(이)가 1445년 영국 레이스스터실르 윈이몽함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Berkeley(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Petronella Brokesby(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1460년 영국 링코실르 슬리퍼드에서 Sir William Hughes Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1504년 12월 21일 영국 링코실르 젬프링함 위스 포인턴 앤드 비르소에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 젬프링함 위스 포인턴 앤드 비르소에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondham nasceu em 1445, em Wymondham, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Berkeley, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Petronella Brokesby, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Hughes Sr em 1460, em Sleaford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 21 de dezembro de 1504, em Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultada em Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Sempringham with Pointon and Birthorpe,: семпрингхам вит поинтон анд биртаурп, линколншир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Berkeley of Wymondham родилась в 1445 г. в местности вимондхам, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Berkeley было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Petronella Brokesby, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Hughes Sr в 1460 г. в местности слифорд, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 21 декабря 1504 г. в местности семпрингхам вит поинтон анд биртаурп, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности семпрингхам вит поинтон анд биртаурп, линколншир, Англия.
